mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 50% of declining rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 65% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE growth exceeds 1.0 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand recovery, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform single-series baselines or produces negative returns in more than 55% of co-expansion periods over a 24-month walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production reflect supply chain bottlenecks and output constraints that trigger short-term equity sell-offs over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of consecutive decline periods, or the signal reverses direction during demand-led expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces central banks to anticipate rate hikes, increasing discount rates and equity valuation compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 58% of high-inflation trigger months, or the correlation between CPI acceleration and equity returns turns positive.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payrolls growth combined with falling unemployment confirms robust labor market tightening and rising corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative returns in more than 50% of occurrences or fails to beat a simple moving average benchmark over 12-month rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding economic output and corporate revenue fundamentals, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 5-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 45% of positive GDP growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production operating above trend alongside accelerating retail sales signals synchronized supply and demand strength, triggering short-term positive S&P 500 momentum over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO exceeds 100 and RSAFS growth > 2% YoY
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 62% of triggered periods, or the signal loses statistical significance when inflation expectations exceed 3%.
